name: Walmart Money Center
description_short: Walmart MoneyCenter offers check cashing, money transfers, bill payments, and prepaid debit cards at Walmart store locations nationwide with extended hours and low fees.
description_long:
Walmart MoneyCenter operates as a financial services hub embedded within Walmart retail locations across the United States and Puerto Rico. Launched to provide banking alternatives for unbanked and underbanked consumers, MoneyCenter functions both as a standalone service center in some stores and as an integrated service available at all Walmart locations. The service addresses a real consumer need: affordable access to financial transactions without requiring a traditional bank account.

Walmart MoneyCenter offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including check cashing (up to $5,000 year-round, $7,500 January-April), two prepaid debit card options (Green Dot Card and Walmart MoneyCard), bill payment services, domestic money transfers via Walmart2Walmart, and international transfers through Walmart2World to over 200 countries. Check cashing accepts pre-printed payroll checks, government benefits, and tax refunds. The Walmart MoneyCard provides cash back rewards (3% at Walmart.com, 2% at fuel stations, 1% in-store up to $75/year), while the Green Dot Card charges $7.95 monthly but waives fees with $500+ in qualifying direct deposits.

What distinguishes Walmart MoneyCenter is its ubiquity and convenience: operating at nearly 5,000 Walmart locations with extended hours (Monday-Saturday 8 AM-8 PM, Sunday 10 AM-6 PM), it provides accessibility that traditional banks cannot match in many communities. The service combines check cashing with money transfer and bill payment capabilities in one location, reducing the need to visit multiple vendors. Additionally, customer service desks can assist with basic transactions outside MoneyCenter hours, and all Walmart locations offer these services whether or not a dedicated center exists.

However, the website itself acknowledges limitations and offers transparent alternatives. For consumers without bank accounts, Walmart recommends exploring traditional banks that now waive monthly fees and offer free transfers via smartphone apps. For those with credit challenges, the site suggests "second chance accounts" as potentially better alternatives to prepaid cards. This honesty about when other options might serve customers better suggests MoneyCenter is best suited for convenience-focused users with immediate, specific transaction needs rather than as a long-term primary financial solution.
address: 6401 NE Loop 820, North Richland Hills, TX 76180

company_info.headquarters: 6401 NE Loop 820, North Richland Hills, TX 76180

pros: Check cashing limits up to $5,000 year-round ($7,500 January-April) for payroll, government, and tax refund checks
Extended operating hours (8 AM-8 PM weekdays/Saturdays, 10 AM-6 PM Sunday) at nearly 5,000 locations nationwide
Walmart MoneyCard offers cash back rewards: 3% on Walmart.com, 2% at fuel stations, 1% in-store purchases
Domestic money transfers (Walmart2Walmart) to US, Puerto Rico, and select Mexico locations with low fees and fast delivery
International transfers (Walmart2World) to over 200 countries available
Green Dot Card waives $7.95 monthly fee with $500+ in qualifying direct deposits
Access to services at all Walmart locations regardless of dedicated MoneyCenter presence
cons: Green Dot Card carries a $7.95 monthly fee (though waivable with qualifying direct deposits)
Website recommends traditional banks as potentially better alternatives for long-term financial needs and free transfers
Check cashing is time-limited to MoneyCenter hours; customer service desk assistance outside these hours limited to 'basic transactions'
Prepaid card rewards capped at $75/year maximum cash back on Walmart MoneyCard
Website suggests 'second chance' bank accounts may be preferable for consumers with banking history issues
best_for: Unbanked consumers needing convenient check cashing without a bank account
Walmart frequent shoppers seeking cash back rewards on purchases and Walmart.com transactions
Consumers needing quick domestic or international money transfers with low fees
Individuals who prefer in-store bill payment over online banking systems
diagnosis: Walmart MoneyCenter is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers seeking convenient, low-cost check cashing and money transfer services at widespread Walmart locations. The primary caveat is that the company's own website recommends traditional banks and credit union alternatives for long-term financial needs, positioning MoneyCenter as a convenience tool for specific transactions rather than a comprehensive banking solution.
services: Check cashing (payroll, government, tax refund checks up to $5,000 year-round/$7,500 Jan-Apr)
Green Dot prepaid debit card with $7.95 monthly fee
Walmart MoneyCard prepaid debit card with cash back rewards
Bill payment services for utilities and other bills
Walmart2Walmart domestic money transfers across US and Puerto Rico
Walmart2World international money transfers to 200+ countries
Prepaid debit card activation and management
Direct deposit setup for fee waiver qualification
Customer service desk assistance for basic transactions
